lede: A look at November 30 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Richard Barbieri and Abel Xavier, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2018 Anchorage earthquake
events.desc: A magnitude 7.1 earthquake with its epicenter only 24 km from Anchorage, Alaska causes significant property damage but no deaths.

events.title: John Sentamu
events.desc: John Sentamu becomes the first black archbishop in the Church of England with his enthronement as the 97th Archbishop of York.

events.title: Bill Clinton
events.desc: U.S. President Bill Clinton visits Northern Ireland and speaks in favor of the "Northern Ireland peace process" to a huge rally at Belfast City Hall; he calls IRA fighters "yesterday's men".

events.title: Mutesa II of Buganda
events.desc: Edward Mutesa II, the kabaka (king) of Buganda is deposed and exiled to London by Sir Andrew Cohen, Governor of Uganda.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Soviet Red Army crosses the Finnish border in several places, bombing Helsinki and several other Finnish cities, starting the Winter War.

events.title: Costa Rica
events.desc: Costa Rica signs the Buenos Aires Convention, a copyright treaty.

events.title: 1872 Scotland v England football match
events.desc: The first-ever international football match takes place at Hamilton Crescent, Glasgow, between Scotland and England.

events.title: Balmis Expedition
events.desc: The Balmis Expedition starts in Spain with the aim of vaccinating millions against smallpox in Spanish America and Philippines.

events.title: Great Northern War
events.desc: Great Northern War: King Charles XII of Sweden dies during a siege of the fortress of Fredriksten in Norway.

births.name: Winston Churchill
births.line: English colonel, journalist, and politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Jiang Zemin, Chinese politician, former General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party (paramount leader) and President of China
George H. W. Bush, American politician, 41st President of the United States and 43rd Vice President of the United States
Marina Popovich, Soviet pilot, engineer and military officer
Oscar Wilde, Irish playwright, novelist, and poet
Charles XII of Sweden, King of Sweden from 1697 to 1718

observances.text: Christian feast day: Andrew and its related observances.
Christian feast day: Joseph Marchand (one of Vietnamese Martyrs)
Day to Mark the Departure and Expulsion of Jews from the Arab Countries and Iran (Israel)
